title: Tender Charred Korean Lamb Kebabs
description: A flavorful and energizing middle-eastern dish featuring gochugaru and ferment-jang, this keto-friendly recipe is perfect for dinner, with tender lamb and a charred exterior.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together gochugaru, ferment-jang, coconut oil, garlic, ginger, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the marinade.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the lamb kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they are charred and cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the lamb kebabs hot, garnished with chopped green onions and a side of cauliflower rice.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 3 days.
